explanation:
**Child development and healthy sleep** are fundamental to growth, learning, and emotional wellbeing. Each age group has distinct sleep needs.

## How It Works

This calculator applies NSF and AAP sleep guidelines based on age, checking that results fall within recommended ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Important Notes

These guidelines are reference recommendations only. For medical decisions, sleep disorders, or persistent issues, consult a pediatrician or sleep specialist. Recommendations are current as of 2026 and updated periodically.

faq.q: How much sleep do newborns need?
faq.a: Newborns (0–3 months): 14–17 hours daily. Infants (4–11 months): 12–15 hours daily, including naps.

faq.q: How many hours should toddlers sleep?
faq.a: Ages 1–2: 11–14 hours daily. Ages 3–5: 10–13 hours daily. Both include nap time.

faq.q: How much sleep do school-age children need?
faq.a: Ages 6–13: 9–11 hours nightly. This age group typically needs only nighttime sleep.

faq.q: How many hours do teenagers need?
faq.a: Ages 14–18: 8–10 hours nightly. Teens often need more than they actually get.
Most children stop regular daytime naps between ages 4–5, though some may rest briefly longer.

faq.q: Why is a consistent sleep schedule important?
faq.a: Regular bedtimes help regulate the body clock, improve sleep quality, and support daytime focus and behavior.
